SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.corrosion

Class NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.corrosion.NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge extends Object implements Serializable
Couples a rigorous electrolyte thermodynamic model into the NorsokM506CorrosionRate standard model.

The bare NorsokM506CorrosionRate model estimates the in-situ pH of the water phase from a correlation (Henry's law constant plus the apparent first dissociation constant of carbonic acid) and needs the user to supply bicarbonate concentration and ionic strength separately. That correlation is a screening approximation. When a calibrated electrolyte equation of state is available (for example SystemElectrolyteCPAstatoil with chemical reaction equilibrium), the aqueous speciation — and therefore the in-situ pH — is known rigorously and consistently with the phase behaviour.

This bridge takes an electrolyte fluid, runs an isolated TP flash on a clone (so the caller's fluid is not mutated), extracts the operating state, and feeds it into a NorsokM506CorrosionRate instance:

  • temperature and total pressure from the fluid state,
  • CO2 (and H2S) mole fraction from the non-aqueous phase,
  • the rigorous in-situ pH from the aqueous phase via SystemInterface.getpH() — this is passed as an explicit override so it takes precedence over the model's own pH correlation,
  • the bicarbonate concentration extracted from the aqueous HCO3- speciation (informational; the pH override already embeds its effect).

This is the NORSOK M-506 analogue of CO2CorrosionAnalyzer, which performs the same electrolyte-to-corrosion coupling for the de Waard-Milliams model. It lets an investigation compute an EOS-consistent CO2 corrosion rate directly from a produced-water brine composition, rather than from hand-entered pH.

Usage example: 

 
 SystemInterface fluid = new SystemElectrolyteCPAstatoil(273.15 + 60.0, 100.0);
 fluid.addComponent("CO2", 0.02);
 fluid.addComponent("water", 0.5);
 fluid.addComponent("methane", 0.48);
 fluid.addComponent("Na+", 0.01);
 fluid.addComponent("Cl-", 0.01);
 fluid.chemicalReactionInit();
 fluid.createDatabase(true);
 fluid.setMixingRule(10);
 fluid.setMultiPhaseCheck(true);

 NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge bridge = new NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge(fluid);
 bridge.setFlowVelocityMs(3.0);
 bridge.setPipeDiameterM(0.254);
 bridge.run();

 double rate = bridge.getModel().getCorrectedCorrosionRate(); // mm/yr
 double pH = bridge.getInSituPH();
 boolean rigorous = bridge.isPHFromElectrolyteModel();
 String json = bridge.toJson();
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:
  • NorsokM506CorrosionRate
  • CO2CorrosionAnalyzer

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private double
    bicarbonateMgL
    Bicarbonate concentration extracted from aqueous speciation, in mg/L.
    private double
    co2MoleFraction
    CO2 mole fraction in the non-aqueous phase.
    private double
    feCO3SaturationRatio
    FeCO3 supersaturation ratio (IAP/Ksp) extracted from aqueous speciation, or -1 if not available.
    private double
    flowVelocityMs
    Flow velocity in m/s (passed through to the corrosion model).
    private SystemInterface
    fluid
    The electrolyte fluid to analyse (not mutated; a clone is flashed internally).
    private boolean
    freeWaterPresent
    Whether a free aqueous (water) phase was found in the flashed fluid.
    private double
    glycolWeightFraction
    Glycol (MEG/DEG) weight fraction in the aqueous phase (0.0 to 1.0).
    private double
    h2sMoleFraction
    H2S mole fraction in the non-aqueous phase.
    private boolean
    hasBeenRun
    Whether run() has completed.
    private double
    inhibitorEfficiency
    Chemical inhibitor efficiency (0.0 = none, 1.0 = perfect).
    private double
    inSituPH
    Rigorous in-situ pH of the aqueous phase (NaN if no free water).
    private static final double
    MM_HCO3_G_MOL
    Molar mass of the bicarbonate ion in g/mol, used to report HCO3- in mg/L.
    private static final double
    MM_WATER_G_MOL
    Molar mass of water in g/mol, used to estimate aqueous solvent moles per litre.
    private NorsokM506CorrosionRate
    model
    The configured and calculated corrosion model.
    private boolean
    pHFromElectrolyteModel
    Whether the pH fed to the corrosion model came from the electrolyte model (true) or the correlation.
    private double
    pipeDiameterM
    Pipe internal diameter in metres (passed through to the corrosion model).
    private static final long

    Serialization version UID.
    private double
    temperatureC
    Temperature extracted from the fluid, in degrees Celsius.
    private double
    totalPressureBara
    Total pressure extracted from the fluid, in bara.
    private static final double
    WATER_DENSITY_KG_L
    Aqueous water density assumption in kg/L used to convert aqueous mole fractions to mg/L.

  • Constructor Details

    • NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge

      public NorsokM506ElectrolyteBridge(SystemInterface fluid)
      Constructs a bridge for the supplied electrolyte fluid.
      Parameters:
      fluid - an electrolyte thermodynamic system (for example SystemElectrolyteCPAstatoil); must not be null

  • Method Details

    • setFlowVelocityMs

      public void setFlowVelocityMs(double velocityMs)
      Sets the flow velocity forwarded to the corrosion model.
      Parameters:
      velocityMs - flow velocity in m/s (valid range: 0 to 30)

    • setPipeDiameterM

      public void setPipeDiameterM(double diameterM)
      Sets the pipe internal diameter forwarded to the corrosion model.
      Parameters:
      diameterM - pipe inner diameter in metres (valid range: 0.01 to 2.0)

    • setInhibitorEfficiency

      public void setInhibitorEfficiency(double efficiency)
      Sets the chemical inhibitor efficiency forwarded to the corrosion model.
      Parameters:
      efficiency - inhibitor efficiency factor (0.0 = no inhibitor, 1.0 = perfect)

    • setGlycolWeightFraction

      public void setGlycolWeightFraction(double weightFraction)
      Sets the glycol (MEG/DEG) weight fraction forwarded to the corrosion model.
      Parameters:
      weightFraction - glycol weight fraction (0.0 to 1.0)

    • run

      public void run()
      Runs the coupled analysis: flashes a clone of the electrolyte fluid, extracts the in-situ pH and gas composition, and calculates the NORSOK M-506 corrosion rate with the rigorous pH.

      The caller's fluid object is not modified; the flash is performed on SystemInterface.clone().

    • extractGasMoleFraction

      private double extractGasMoleFraction(SystemInterface system, String componentName)
      Extracts the mole fraction of a component in the first non-aqueous phase (gas or oil/dense).
      Parameters:
      system - the flashed fluid
      componentName - the component name to look up (for example "CO2")
      Returns:
      the mole fraction in the non-aqueous phase, or 0.0 if absent

    • extractBicarbonateMgL

      private double extractBicarbonateMgL(SystemInterface system)
      Estimates the bicarbonate concentration in the aqueous phase in mg/L from the HCO3- mole fraction.

      The estimate uses the aqueous water mole fraction to approximate the solvent mass per litre, then converts the HCO3- mole fraction to a mass concentration. This is an informational output; the pH override already embeds the rigorous carbonate speciation.

      Parameters:
      system - the flashed fluid
      Returns:
      bicarbonate concentration in mg/L, or 0.0 if HCO3- or water is absent

    • computeFeCO3SaturationRatio

      private double computeFeCO3SaturationRatio(SystemInterface system)
      Computes the FeCO3 (siderite) supersaturation ratio SR = IAP / Ksp from the aqueous Fe2+ and CO3-- molalities.

      The temperature-dependent siderite solubility product uses the Sun, Nesic and Woollam (2009) correlation on the molal scale, with Davies-style ionic-strength terms: 

       
 log10(Ksp) = -59.3498 - 0.041377 * T - 2.1963 / T + 24.5724 * log10(T) + 2.518 * sqrt(I) - 0.657 * I

      where T is in Kelvin and I is the ionic strength in mol/kg. The ion activity product is taken as the product of the Fe2+ and CO3-- molalities. Returns -1 when the aqueous phase or either ion is absent, so the caller leaves the FeCO3 film feedback disabled.

      Parameters:
      system - the flashed fluid
      Returns:
      the FeCO3 supersaturation ratio, or -1 if it cannot be computed

    • getModel

      public NorsokM506CorrosionRate getModel()
      Gets the configured and calculated corrosion model.
      Returns:
      the NorsokM506CorrosionRate instance (null until run() is called)

    • getInSituPH

      public double getInSituPH()
      Gets the rigorous in-situ pH extracted from the aqueous phase.
      Returns:
      in-situ pH, or NaN if no free water phase was present

    • getCo2MoleFraction

      public double getCo2MoleFraction()
      Gets the CO2 mole fraction extracted from the non-aqueous phase.
      Returns:
      CO2 mole fraction (0 to 1)

    • getH2sMoleFraction

      public double getH2sMoleFraction()
      Gets the H2S mole fraction extracted from the non-aqueous phase.
      Returns:
      H2S mole fraction (0 to 1)

    • getBicarbonateMgL

      public double getBicarbonateMgL()
      Gets the bicarbonate concentration extracted from aqueous speciation.
      Returns:
      bicarbonate concentration in mg/L

    • getFeCO3SaturationRatio

      public double getFeCO3SaturationRatio()
      Gets the FeCO3 (siderite) supersaturation ratio extracted from the aqueous speciation.
      Returns:
      FeCO3 supersaturation ratio (IAP/Ksp), or -1 if it could not be computed

    • isFreeWaterPresent

      public boolean isFreeWaterPresent()
      Indicates whether a free aqueous (water) phase was present in the flashed fluid.
      Returns:
      true if free water was present, false otherwise

    • isPHFromElectrolyteModel

      public boolean isPHFromElectrolyteModel()
      Indicates whether the pH fed to the corrosion model came from the electrolyte model.
      Returns:
      true if the rigorous in-situ pH was used, false if the built-in correlation was used

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serialises the extracted state and the corrosion result to a JSON string.
      Returns:
      a pretty-printed JSON representation of the bridge result
